title: setaria humbertiana
intro: Setaria humbertiana, also known as Humbert's foxtail, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Madagascar, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In traditional herbal preparations, Setaria humbertiana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
